acceptance.metric: 1. Body mesh frame trust_badge schema/export/index round-trip
acceptance.baseline: BodyMesh frames and body_mesh_index frames had no optional per-frame trust_badge field; present extra fields would drift against the schema.
acceptance.after: BodyMesh accepts optional trust_badge values from trust_band.py vocabulary, legacy absent-field payloads still validate, body_mesh_export emits preview for human_review scheduled frames, preserves verified, and body_mesh_index round-trips the field.
acceptance.target: Optional trust_badge with verified | preview | low_confidence vocabulary round-trips without breaking old artifacts.

acceptance.baseline: human_review/manual_review_required frames were excluded from mesh eligibility and skipped from BODY mesh scheduling.
acceptance.after: ProcessVideoPipeline events stage selects human_review frames into the byte-budget mesh set and writes trust_badge=preview on those selected frame_compute_plan records; process_video auto-court/no-contact path also emits preview ghost mesh records.
acceptance.target: human_review-tier frames are emitted as ghost/estimated mesh candidates, never hidden or solid, through scripts/racketsport/process_video.py plumbing.

acceptance.baseline: Non-human-review mesh frames did not carry a trust_badge and legacy payloads omitted the field.
acceptance.after: Explicit verified trust_badge values are preserved, legacy absent trust_badge stays absent/valid, and non-human-review selected frames are not force-badged by the eligibility change.
acceptance.target: verified frames unchanged; absent-field back-compat preserved.

acceptance.baseline: human_review ghost frames were not counted as eligible mesh frames because they were excluded before byte-budget selection.
acceptance.after: Byte-budget test records eligible_mesh_frame_count=3, selected_mesh_frame_count=2, selected_estimated_mesh_bytes within mesh_byte_budget_bytes, and both selected human_review frames carry trust_badge=preview.
acceptance.target: Ghost frames count like any other eligible mesh frame; no silent tier re-weighting.

acceptance.baseline: configs/racketsport/best_stack.json had no wired-default entry for human_review ghost mesh emission; mesh.tier_eligibility_raise remained pending.
acceptance.after: best_stack revision 6 adds mesh.human_review_ghost_emission as WIRED_DEFAULT with trust_badge=preview and counts_against_byte_budget=true; mesh.tier_eligibility_raise remains PENDING and 300-vs-400 remains unresolved.
acceptance.target: Ghost-mesh emission is the default path while the wider tier-eligibility raise and byte cap ruling remain pending.

full_suite.failure_notes: Post-patch full suite failed only tests/racketsport/test_body_runner_real_path.py::test_body_runner_fails_loudly_on_detector_sha_mismatch and tests/racketsport/test_truthful_capabilities.py::test_markdown_doc_inventory_stays_small_and_explicit. The detector-SHA failure is unrelated to trust_badge/human_review logic: the fixture is pure deep_mesh, tests/racketsport/test_body_runner_real_path.py was not edited, and this lane only refactored the existing deep_mesh scheduled-frame payload to add an optional trust_badge when present; I did not prove it against a clean detached HEAD checkout. The markdown inventory failure is caused by unrelated untracked files cvat_upload/exports/court_keypoints_20260707/PARTIAL_EXPORT_NOTES_20260709.md and cvat_upload/exports/w6_labelpack_20260708/SESSION_NOTES_20260709.md outside this lane.

honest_issues: objective_result is PARTIAL solely because the full tests/racketsport suite is still red; acceptance targets for this lane passed in focused verification.

Earlier crash census reported 8 visible failures before ENOSPC; on current main 78b8b68a6 the post-patch full run reproduced only 2 failures. The earlier 36%, 56%, and 80% clusters did not reproduce after concurrent-lane churn and the stale lane-related 56% expectation was fixed.

Detector-SHA fail-closed test still expects body_compute_execution scheduled_frame_count=0 but current code writes one deep_mesh scheduled frame before the detector SHA mismatch fails. This is not part of the trust_badge change, but it is not proven pre-existing by a clean HEAD rerun.
Markdown inventory test fails because unrelated untracked CVAT markdown notes are present outside runs/lanes/w7_tierprov_20260709/.
The worktree contains unrelated dirty state, including configs/ssh/a100_known_hosts and tracked CVAT prelabel zip zeroing; these were not changed by this lane and were not reverted.
next: Clear or isolate the unrelated detector-SHA fail-closed expectation and CVAT markdown inventory drift, then rerun the full tests/racketsport suite; no further tierprov implementation work is known from the focused acceptance tests.
